text.title: Microservices Circuit Breaker Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for adding a circuit breaker to a cross-service call.

rule.statement: Every cross-service network call MUST be wrapped in one named circuit breaker. Sharing a breaker across N downstream targets is forbidden.
rule.rationale: A shared breaker conflates failure signals — slow target A opens the breaker for fast target B.

rule.statement: Breaker config MUST set explicit `failureRateThreshold`, `slidingWindowSize`, `minimumNumberOfCalls`, `slowCallRateThreshold`, and `waitDurationInOpenState`. Library defaults are forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Defaults are tuned for synthetic benchmarks; production traffic profile demands explicit tuning.

rule.statement: Every breaker-wrapped call MUST declare a fallback function. The fallback MUST return a valid response shape (cached value, sentinel, or degraded data).
rule.rationale: Without fallback the breaker just rethrows; caller still needs error handling everywhere.

rule.statement: Breaker MUST expose state transitions (closed/open/half-open) + failure rate as metrics; alert on open state > 60s and on flapping (>3 transitions/5min).
rule.rationale: Open breaker = degraded UX. Flapping = misconfigured thresholds.

rule.statement: Retry MUST be inside the breaker (retry → call → breaker counts the final attempt) AND retry MUST have backoff + jitter + max-attempts. Naked retry around a breaker call is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Outer retry around breaker bypasses the open state and amplifies load on a struggling downstream.
